algorithms.title: Pediatric Trauma Primary Assessment with Injury-Pattern Awareness
algorithms.description: Sequence for assessing a pediatric trauma patient with attention to pediatric-specific anatomic vulnerabilities.
steps: Ensure scene safety and control obvious hemorrhage.
Perform primary assessment (ABCDE), watching for pediatric patterns: head injury dominance, chest injury without rib fractures, occult abdominal injury despite a soft exam.
Assess perfusion directly (heart rate, capillary refill, skin) rather than relying on blood pressure alone — significant blood loss can occur with a normal BP.
Check the fontanelle (infants) and trend mental status for evolving head injury.
Note any rib fracture as a marker of high-energy mechanism requiring heightened suspicion for chest injury.
Apply selective spinal motion restriction criteria, weighting mechanism and mental status heavily given limited reliability of pain reporting in young children.
Transport rapidly to an appropriate, ideally pediatric-capable, trauma center with continuous reassessment.

algorithms.title: Pediatric Spinal Motion Restriction Positioning
algorithms.description: Steps for maintaining neutral spinal alignment in a young child requiring spinal motion restriction.
steps: Determine the need for spinal motion restriction using selective criteria (mechanism, mental status, distracting injury, midline tenderness, neurologic complaint), weighting mechanism/mental status heavily in young children.
If restriction is indicated, prepare the backboard/device and recognize that a young child's proportionally larger head will force neck flexion if placed flat without adjustment.
Add padding under the torso (not just the head) to raise the torso relative to the head and restore neutral alignment.
Secure the child appropriately for age/size, avoiding excessive restriction that could compromise breathing.
Reassess neurovascular status and comfort throughout transport.
